---
title: "Results"
author: "abi :)"
date: "2026-04-03"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)     
library(ggplot2)   
library(dplyr)     
library(stringr)   
library(fs)        
```

# INDIVIDUAL BIOMASS CURVES

```{r}
biomass_files <- sort(
  list.files(
    path = "/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/biomass/individuales/lb",
    pattern = "prokka_carveme_lb_lb\\.csv$", 
    full.names = TRUE
  )
)

output_folder <- "/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/graph"
dir_create(output_folder)

# ----------------------------------------------------
# 4. Loop por archivo (una figura por cepa)
# ----------------------------------------------------
for (input_file in biomass_files) {
  

  model_id <- str_extract(basename(input_file), "^[^_]+")
  
  if (!model_id %in% names(colores_bac)) {
    warning(paste("ID not found in dictionary, skipping:", model_id))
    next
  }
  
  # Leer datos
  df <- read_csv(input_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
  
  growth_data <- tibble(
    time_h  = seq(0, by = 0.1, length.out = nrow(df)), 
    biomass = df[[2]]
  )
  
  # Gráfica individual
  p <- ggplot(growth_data, aes(x = time_h, y = biomass)) +
    geom_line(
      linewidth = 1.3,
      color = colores_bac[model_id]
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste(
        "Individual Growth Curve (48 h) –",
        name_bac[model_id]
      ),
      x = "Time (h)",
      y = expression(paste(italic("Biomass"), " (g)"))
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold.italic"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
  
  # Guardar figura
  output_file <- file.path(
    output_folder,
    paste0("individual_growth_", model_id, "prokka_carveme_lb_lb.png")
  )
  
  ggsave(
    filename = output_file,
    plot = p,
    width = 10,
    height = 7,
    dpi = 300
  )
}
```

# Compare growth media

```{r}
# ----------------------------------------------------
# 1. Setup paths and files
# ----------------------------------------------------
mets_files <- sort(
  list.files(
    path = "/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/mets/individuales",
    pattern = "_mets\\.csv$", 
    full.names = TRUE
  )
)

output_folder <- "/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/graph"
if (!dir.exists(output_folder)) dir.create(output_folder, recursive = TRUE)

# ----------------------------------------------------
# 3. Loop by file
# ----------------------------------------------------
for (input_file in mets_files) {
  
  # Extract ID
  model_id <- str_extract(basename(input_file), "^[^_]+")
  
  if (!model_id %in% names(colores_bac)) {
    warning(paste("ID not found in dictionary, skipping:", model_id))
    next
  }
  
  # Read and format data
  df <- read_csv(input_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
  
  # Ensure we are using the correct dataframe name
  plot_df <- tibble(
    time_h  = seq(0, by = 0.1, length.out = nrow(df)), 
    concentration = df[[2]]  # Assuming metabolite concentration is column 2
  )
  
  # Generate Plot
  p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = time_h, y = concentration)) +
    geom_line(
      linewidth = 1.3,
      color = colores_bac[model_id]
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Metabolite Profile –", name_bac[model_id]),
      subtitle = paste("Model:", model_id),
      x = "Time (h)",
      y = "Concentration (mmol/L)" # Adjust units as necessary for your GEMs
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold.italic"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
  
  # Save
  output_file <- file.path(output_folder, paste0("individual_mets_", model_id, ".png"))
  
  ggsave(
    filename = output_file,
    plot = p,
    width = 10,
    height = 7,
    dpi = 300
  )
}
```



```{r}
library(tidyverse)

# 1. Load the data
df <- read_csv("/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/mets/individuales/ST00042_mets.csv")

# 2. Reshape and Plot
df %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -1, names_to = "Metabolite", values_to = "Value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = .[[1]], y = Value, color = Metabolite)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(x = "Time", title = "Metabolite Concentrations") +
  theme_minimal()
```
